system: You are a spam / phishing classifier for an enterprise B2B SalesOps inbox at Keysight Technologies. Given an email's subject, sender, and translated body, decide whether it is spam or phishing. Most legitimate inbound mail is from established customers asking about purchase orders, calibration, work orders, service contracts, or product information. Spam / phishing signals: unsolicited promo blasts, fake-discount language, urgency pressure ('act now', 'today only'), credential phishing ('verify your account'), payment-redirect scams ('our banking details have changed'), wire-fraud setups, and lookalike-domain senders. Return strict JSON: {"is_spam": bool, "confidence": float 0..1, "reasoning": short string citing specific cues, "category": one of [legitimate, promotional, phishing, payment_redirect, social_engineering, other]}.
user:
FROM: ee.procurement@auroraauto.com
SUBJECT: Convert quote QT-AURA-AUTO-119-DEMO → order — PO + BOM + ATP attached
BODY (translated to English):
Hello team,

Please convert quote QT-AURA-AUTO-119-DEMO into an order using the attached PO PO-AURA-AUTO-119-Q2O-72296, BOM workbook, and acceptance test plan. The instruments will support our automotive Ethernet 100BASE-T1 conformance + ISO 26262 functional safety program with delivery into our automotive Ethernet / 100BASE-T1 conformance bench.

Send SOA, projected ship date, and confirm cal certs will be A2LA-traceable as quoted.

Thanks,
Danielle Park, Engineering Procurement
Aurora Automotive Electronics

Return JSON only.
raw_response: {"is_spam":false,"confidence":0.96,"reasoning":"B2B procurement request from a corporate domain asking to convert an existing quote to an order; references PO, BOM, acceptance test plan, ship date, and A2LA-traceable calibration certificates—typical legitimate SalesOps workflow with no promo/urgency/credential or payment-change cues.","category":"legitimate"}

parsed.reasoning: B2B procurement request from a corporate domain asking to convert an existing quote to an order; references PO, BOM, acceptance test plan, ship date, and A2LA-traceable calibration certificates—typical legitimate SalesOps workflow with no promo/urgency/credential or payment-change cues.
